query: Our marketing team is pushing back on how concentrated our crop mix is — they want a diversification rule: no single crop's tonnage can exceed 30% of total crop production. How does that hit farm income?
ground_truth_constraint:
xprod[cotton-h]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[banana]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[sugar-cane]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[beans-arr]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[beans-cor]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[oranges]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[manioc]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[corn]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[sisal]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
optichat_constraint:
xprod[cotton-h]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[banana]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[sugar-cane]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[beans-arr]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[beans-cor]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[oranges]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[manioc]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[corn]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])
xprod[sisal]  <=  0.3*(xprod[cotton-h] + xprod[banana] + xprod[sugar-cane] + xprod[beans-arr] + xprod[beans-cor] + xprod[oranges] + xprod[manioc] + xprod[corn] + xprod[sisal])

query: Temporary labor spikes in peak months while family labor sits idle in others. Shouldn't a smoother plan beat the current one? Couldn't we require that in every month, temporary labor never makes up more than 25% of the total labor used that month (family plus temporary)?
ground_truth_constraint:
tlab[jan]  <=  0.25*(tlab[jan] + flab[jan])
tlab[feb]  <=  0.25*(tlab[feb] + flab[feb])
tlab[mar]  <=  0.25*(tlab[mar] + flab[mar])
tlab[apr]  <=  0.25*(tlab[apr] + flab[apr])
tlab[may]  <=  0.25*(tlab[may] + flab[may])
tlab[jun]  <=  0.25*(tlab[jun] + flab[jun])
tlab[jul]  <=  0.25*(tlab[jul] + flab[jul])
tlab[aug]  <=  0.25*(tlab[aug] + flab[aug])
tlab[sep]  <=  0.25*(tlab[sep] + flab[sep])
tlab[oct]  <=  0.25*(tlab[oct] + flab[oct])
tlab[nov]  <=  0.25*(tlab[nov] + flab[nov])
tlab[dec]  <=  0.25*(tlab[dec] + flab[dec])
optichat_constraint:
tlab[jan]  <=  0.25*(flab[jan] + tlab[jan] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[feb]  <=  0.25*(flab[feb] + tlab[feb] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[mar]  <=  0.25*(flab[mar] + tlab[mar] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[apr]  <=  0.25*(flab[apr] + tlab[apr] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[may]  <=  0.25*(flab[may] + tlab[may] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[jun]  <=  0.25*(flab[jun] + tlab[jun] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[jul]  <=  0.25*(flab[jul] + tlab[jul] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[aug]  <=  0.25*(flab[aug] + tlab[aug] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[sep]  <=  0.25*(flab[sep] + tlab[sep] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[oct]  <=  0.25*(flab[oct] + tlab[oct] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[nov]  <=  0.25*(flab[nov] + tlab[nov] + 25.0*plab)
tlab[dec]  <=  0.25*(flab[dec] + tlab[dec] + 25.0*plab)
counterexample:
[flab[jun] = 0,
 tlab[jan] = 2/3,
 tlab[jul] = 2/3,
 tlab[jun] = 2/3,
 flab[jan] = 0,
 tlab[aug] = 2/3,
 flab[mar] = 0,
 tlab[mar] = 2/3,
 flab[aug] = 0,
 flab[sep] = 0,
 plab = 2/25,
 tlab[sep] = 2/3,
 tlab[apr] = 2/3,
 flab[dec] = 0,
 tlab[feb] = 2/3,
 tlab[dec] = 2/3,
 tlab[nov] = 2/3,
 flab[jul] = 0,
 flab[oct] = 0,
 flab[nov] = 0,
 flab[may] = 0,
 tlab[oct] = 2/3,
 flab[apr] = 0,
 tlab[may] = 2/3,
 flab[feb] = 0]

query: Total labor cost looks high relative to what we bring in. Is there a reason the plan doesn't hold total labor cost at no more than a quarter of total revenue? Wouldn't that ratio produce a healthier-looking plan?
